title: Default datagrid height not adjustable
symptom: The datagrid maintains a default height even when set to 'auto', causing layout issues with fewer rows.
not_this_if: Datagrid height is correctly set in custom code
root_cause: The AG-grid library used for the datagrid has a default configuration that enforces a minimum height, which does not adjust based on the number of rows present in the grid.
fix_steps: Go to More / Custom code in your app settings.
Add the following CSS code: .ag-center-cols-viewport { min-height: unset !important; }
Alternatively, add an HTML element to your page with the same CSS code.
agent_instruction: Add custom CSS to adjust the datagrid's minimum height as needed.
